title: Bridge Essay: Effects of long-term dharma-chan meditation on cardiorespiratory synchronization

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Heat/Fire** (Phase 3) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Heat/Fire to mechanistic neuroscience through Effects, of, long-term, dharma-chan, meditation.

**Effects of long-term dharma-chan meditation on cardiorespiratory synchronization and heart rate variability behavior.** — Rejuvenation Res. PMID: 23323597. DOI: 10.1089/rej.2012.1363. Remarkable changes in cardiorespiratory interactions are frequently experienced by Chan meditation practitioners following years of practice. This study compares the results of our study on cardiorespiratory interactions for novice (control group) and experienced (experimental group) Chan meditation practitioners. The effectual co-action between the cardiac and respiratory systems was evaluated by the degree of cardiorespiratory phase synchronization (CRPS). In addition, an adaptive-frequency-ra
